lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS231N Lecture 2: Image Classification with Linear Classifiers

unit.name: Hyperparameters, validation, and why pixel-space k-NN breaks
unit.description: Uses k-NN to introduce hyperparameters and sound evaluation practice, then shows empirically why nearest neighbor on raw pixels is not a practical image classifier.
unit.summary: The segment motivates nearest neighbor as a vehicle for understanding hyperparameters: both K and the distance metric must be chosen for the dataset at hand [ts=2140s][ts=2165s][ts=2181s][ts=2193s]. It warns against two bad selection strategies: tuning on training data, which favors k=1 because the training set is memorized [ts=2224s][ts=2254s][ts=2263s], and tuning on the test set, which leaks information and destroys a trustworthy estimate of generalization [ts=2271s][ts=2291s][ts=2310s]. The recommended workflow is train/validation/test splitting, optionally extended to cross-validation when data is limited [ts=2321s][ts=2339s][ts=2392s][ts=2412s][ts=2444s]. On CIFAR-10, a 5-fold sweep over k suggests roughly k=7 and only about 28-29% accuracy, better than random guessing but still weak [ts=2505s][ts=2572s][ts=2581s][ts=2597s]. Visual nearest-neighbor examples reveal the core failure mode: raw pixel distances can make semantically wrong images look close, so pixel-space k-NN is not used in practice for image classification [ts=2610s][ts=2633s][ts=2650s][ts=2674s][ts=2700s].

key_points.text: The choice of K and the choice of distance metric are hyperparameters.
Choosing hyperparameters on the training set is bad because k=1 can memorize the training data.
Choosing hyperparameters on the test set is also bad because it leaks evaluation information.
Cross-validation averages validation performance across folds to pick hyperparameters more reliably.
On CIFAR-10, about k=7 performs best in the shown 5-fold experiment, at roughly 28-29% accuracy.
Pixel-space nearest neighbor is not used in practice because semantically different images can have similar pixel distances.

kp_catalog.name: Distance metrics for similarity
kp_catalog.description: The choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because distance metrics for similarity clarifies the choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Hyperparameter tuning and search
kp_catalog.description: A practical workflow for validating a training setup, choosing learning rates, reading train/validation curves, and searching hyperparameters efficiently. Model settings such as K and distance metric must be selected using held-out validation procedures rather than training or test data.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because hyperparameter tuning and search clarifies a practical workflow for validating a training setup, choosing learning rates, reading train/validation curves, and searching hyperparameters efficiently, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices though importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Failure of pixel-space nearest neighbors
kp_catalog.description: Raw pixel distance is not semantically meaningful for images, so visually wrong neighbors can appear close and tiny transformations can break similarity.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because failure of pixel-space nearest neighbors clarifies raw pixel distance is not semantically meaningful for images, so visually wrong neighbors can appear close and tiny transformations can break similarity, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
